tasks.title: Lease notice memo
tasks.instructions: Write a short memo stating whether notice is required and how it may be sent.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Lease notice memo

The tenant should send written notice within ten calendar days after learning of the material service interruption. Email can satisfy the notice requirement if the landlord acknowledges receipt.

tasks.title: Purchase agreement indemnity memo
tasks.instructions: Write a short answer identifying the indemnity trigger and claim-notice deadline.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Purchase indemnity answer

The seller indemnifies the buyer for pre-closing tax liabilities. The buyer must send a claim notice within thirty days after discovering the liability.

tasks.title: Privilege log classification
tasks.instructions: Classify the two privilege-log entries and explain the likely privilege result.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Privilege log classification

Entry 14 is likely privileged because outside counsel sent litigation strategy to the general counsel. Entry 17 is likely not privileged on these facts because it is an ordinary business report copied to counsel.

tasks.title: Employment noncompete risk memo
tasks.instructions: Write a concise memo assessing enforceability risk and next facts needed before sending a cease-and-desist letter.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Employment noncompete risk memo

The noncompete is high risk because California generally bars employee noncompetes for ordinary employees. Before sending a letter, confirm governing law, trade-secret facts, equity-sale facts, and any confidentiality or nonsolicit provisions that may be enforceable.

tasks.title: Privacy breach notice triage
tasks.instructions: Identify likely notice issues, timing pressure, and missing facts for breach response.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Privacy breach notice triage

The incident involves personal information for California residents and account tokens, so counsel should assess state breach notice and consumer notice obligations promptly. Needed facts include whether tokens enabled account access, whether accounts were misused, exact discovery date, and whether any contractual or regulator notice deadlines apply.

tasks.title: Credit agreement covenant calculation
tasks.instructions: Calculate the covenant ratio and state whether the borrower appears compliant.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Credit agreement covenant calculation

The debt service coverage ratio is 9.0 million divided by 8.0 million, or 1.125 to 1.00. That is below the required 1.25 to 1.00, so the borrower appears noncompliant unless adjustments or cure rights apply.

tasks.title: IP license termination memo
tasks.instructions: State whether termination is available and what date matters.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# IP license termination memo

Termination becomes available only if the missed payment is a material breach and remains uncured 15 days after the February 1 written notice. The key date is February 16, subject to the agreement's notice and cure-counting rules.

tasks.title: Arbitration clause enforceability memo
tasks.instructions: Assess the arbitration clause risk and identify the clause feature that creates enforceability concern.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Arbitration clause enforceability memo

The class waiver and individual arbitration terms may support a motion to compel, but unilateral company control over arbitrator selection creates unconscionability risk. Needed facts include governing law, delegation language, opt-out rights, fee terms, and how arbitrators are actually selected.

tasks.title: NDA residuals risk memo
tasks.instructions: Explain the risk created by the residuals clause and what facts matter before advising the recipient.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# NDA residuals risk memo

The residuals clause may let personnel use unaided memory, but it is risky for roadmap and customer pricing details because those facts may still be confidential or trade-secret sensitive. Needed facts include who saw the information, whether notes were used, overlap between features, and whether the NDA separately restricts reverse engineering or customer use.

tasks.title: Board consent validity memo
tasks.instructions: Determine whether the written consent likely approved the financing and what defect matters.
tasks.replay_answer_markdown:
# Board consent validity memo

The two-director written consent likely did not validly approve the financing because the bylaws require either majority approval of the entire five-member board or unanimous written consent. The defect is both the number of approving directors and the lack of unanimous written consent.
